Title: timeOmics: an R package for longitudinal multi-omics data integration
Author: Bodein, A.; Scott-Boyer, M.P.; Perin, O.; et al.
Keywords: multi-omics, data integration, longitudinal, R package
Abstract: Introduces a generic analytical framework for the integration of longitudinal multi-omics data (mRNA, metabolites, etc.) to identify molecular features associated with time.

Title: Testing the effectiveness of community-engaged citizen science to promote physical activity, foster healthier neighborhood environments, and advance health equity in vulnerable communities: The Steps for Change randomized controlled trial design and methods
Author: King, Abby C.; Campero, Maria, I; Garcia, Dulce; et al.
Keywords: citizen science, physical activity, health equity, RCT
Abstract: A cluster-randomized controlled trial design comparing person-level physical activity interventions against citizen science-driven neighborhood interventions.

Title: Space-Based Global Maritime Surveillance. Part II: AI and Data Fusion Techniques
Author: Soldi, G.; Gaglione, D.; Forti, N.; et al.
Publication Title: IEEE AEROSPACE AND ELECTRONIC SYSTEMS MAGAZINE
Keywords: maritime surveillance, AI, data fusion, satellite data
Abstract: Part II of a study focusing on artificial intelligence and data fusion techniques specifically for global maritime tracking using space-based sensors.

Title: A Prioritized and Validated Resource of Mitochondrial Proteins in Plasmodium...
Author: van Esveld, S. L.; Meerstein-Kessel, L.; et al.
Abstract: Integrates eight diverse data sets including gene expression, orthology, and amino acid sequences into a predictive score for protein localization.

Title: Proposal-Copula-Based Fusion of Spaceborne and Airborne SAR Images...
Author: Li, X.; Wang, R.; Deng, Y.; et al.
Keywords: image fusion, SAR, copula theory, ship detection
Abstract: Proposes a new method (TPCT) for fusing synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images from spaceborne and airborne sensors to improve target-to-clutter ratios.
